_comment: Root manifest for the Ex-Ditector (Self Footprint Audit Pro) Apify project. This is a descriptive manifest of the multi-actor system + the platform integrations this product wires in. It contains NO credentials and asserts NO live deployment; all usernames/ids/secrets are placeholders supplied by the operator at deploy time.
name: ex-ditector
title: Ex-Ditector — Self Footprint Audit Pro (compliant)
description: Audits a user's OWN public digital footprint, preserves public evidence involving themselves, and reduces compulsive checking (Closure Mode). The opposite of an ex-stalking tool. Every run is gated by shared/scope.js (scope_type: self | consented | public_figure | brand | safety_evidence).

discovery.role: A2 enumerate public sources for the vetted subject

crawler.role: A3 adaptive crawl + evidence capture, compliance backoff

report-builder.role: A6 compliant scoring + Closure Mode report

ingest.capability: Apify Website Content Crawler + RAG Web Browser as the scope-gated SELF-AUDIT ingestion source
ingest.summary: The REAL ingestion layer: a vetted subject's OWN public footprint is fetched via apify/website-content-crawler (page crawl -> clean text/markdown + htmlUrl/screenshotUrl evidence handles + error on failed pages) and contextualized via apify/rag-web-browser (public Google search OR single-URL fetch -> top-N markdown). buildIngestPlan() runs the REAL shared/scope.js gate FIRST (read-only) and refuses to build ANY actor input for a rejected subject, so a private-individual / stalking query is DROPPED before a single fetch input exists. A RAG free-text/name search is a dual-use discovery chokepoint, allowed only for scope=self|public_figure (consented/brand/safety_evidence must pass a concrete URL). The WCC input-builder FORCES respectRobotsTxtFile:true (anti-evasion) and clamps maxCrawlPages/Depth DOWN to caps (anti-dragnet), and confines the crawl to the subject's own hosts via includeUrlGlobs. A real Scrapy-style item-pipeline (shared/middleware) maps each REAL crawler row -> detector ARTIFACT_KINDS -> shared/detectors module_events -> shared/enrich/stix-evidence STIX 2.1 Observed Data (first_observed/last_observed/content-hash/observable category), re-asserting host on every row so a link/metamorph cannot smuggle a non-vetted host. NO-FAKE-DATA: the builder only builds the exact input an operator would submit and the mapper only transforms REAL rows passed to it; without APIFY_TOKEN the client dry-runs (started:false, no network, no fabricated rows). actorIds are placeholders and deployed:false until the operator wires a live token (the LAST step).

env.APIFY_TOKEN: run token for POST /v2/acts/{actorId}/run-sync-get-dataset-items. Without it, ingest-client.js DRY-RUNS and returns the exact request it WOULD send (never live, never fabricated rows).

ingest.maps_to: shared/detectors (ARTIFACT_KINDS) -> module_events -> shared/enrich/stix-evidence (STIX 2.1 Observed Data, OpenCTI/MISP interop)

standby.capability: Apify Actor Standby (real-time HTTP API) + Metamorph actor chain
standby.summary: Turns the policy gate into a warm, real-time API. POST /inspect takes ONE subject, runs the REAL shared/scope.js gate, and only on a pass metamorphs the run into the audit pipeline (policy-gate -> discovery -> crawler -> diff-evidence -> report-builder, all in the same run, storages preserved). A rejected subject returns HTTP 403 with legal alternatives and NEVER starts a run. Single entry point: a request cannot start mid-chain (gate_bypass_attempt), and every stage re-asserts scope so a metamorph cannot smuggle a refused subject downstream (SpiderFoot module-edge gating). The one-subject endpoint shape mirrors The Markup Blacklight's single real-time self-exposure inspector. NO-FAKE-DATA: the real Actor.metamorph fires ONLY on-platform (APIFY_IS_AT_HOME=1) against a real actorId; off-platform the endpoint returns the plan with started:false and performs no side effect.

env.ACTOR_WEB_SERVER_PORT: port Apify Standby proxies user requests to; server.js binds it (default 4321 local).
env.APIFY_IS_AT_HOME: "1" on the Apify platform; gates the real Actor.metamorph. Off-platform the gate still runs but metamorph is inert.
env.APIFY_STANDBY_URL: client.js target; unset => dry-run (local plan, no request, no run started).
env.APIFY_TOKEN: bearer token client.js sends to the Standby endpoint.

webhooks.capability: Apify run Webhooks with an OUTPUT-HEALTH receiver
webhooks.summary: Subscribes to ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED/FAILED/TIMED_OUT/ABORTED and verifies that a clean 'SUCCEEDED' actually produced real, compliant output before claiming an audit is ready. Treats all-backoff runs as a COMPLIANCE STOP (human review), empty/malformed output as NOT ready. Implements the 'success != valid output' rule against the product's NO-FAKE-DATA standard.

env.APIFY_TOKEN: read-only; lets the receiver fetch dataset/OUTPUT to judge health. Without it, health = UNKNOWN (never guessed up).
env.APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET: URL secret token; receiver verifies path /apify-webhook/<secret>.
env.APIFY_WEBHOOK_HMAC_SECRET: optional HMAC-SHA256 secret over the raw body (defense in depth).

schedules.capability: Apify Schedules (cron) for PACED, anti-compulsion re-audits
schedules.summary: Recurring re-audits driven by a fail-closed policy engine (cadence-policy.js) instead of a person hitting refresh. Embodies Closure Mode in cron: there is NO high-frequency option, and a higher distress_risk_score yields a SLOWER cadence, never faster. The effective interval is always the slower of (requested cadence, distress floor). Scheduling is restricted to scope=self|public_figure (a dual-use chokepoint, fail-closed); consented/brand/safety_evidence cannot be auto-scheduled. cron is always re-derived from policy, never trusted from config (Scrapy item-pipeline drop pattern). Schedule names carry only a short non-identifying subject_token (HIBP k-anonymity stance), never an email/handle/name.

env.APIFY_TOKEN: write token for POST /v2/schedules. Without it, register-schedules.js dry-runs and prints the exact body (never live).

exports.capability: Apify Dataset Views + marking-scoped Export API
exports.summary: Governs how audit data LEAVES the platform. Declares native dataset views (transformation+display in .actor/actor.json) and a real GET /v2/datasets/{id}/items export client (format/fields/omit/clean/flatten), both bound to a STIX/MISP-style distribution marking. Fail-closed redaction: raw locators (url/html_key/screenshot_key/note/subject_label) NEVER leave TLP:RED; shareable bands keep only content/html HASHES, coarse status, change flags and timestamps. The export fields/omit are DERIVED from policy, never trusted from the caller; an unknown marking is refused, not widened. Closure-Mode friendly: a shared summary proves THAT something changed without a clickable destination.

portable_evidence_package.format: Frictionless Tabular Data Package (datapackage.json + per-record-type CSV resources with Table Schemas)
portable_evidence_package.purpose: The subject OWNS their evidence: a self-contained, offline-openable, Datasette/SQLite/pandas-importable, citable bundle. WRAPS redaction-policy.js (never bypasses): every row is run through redactRecord(row, marking) first, so a non-RED package physically cannot carry url/storage-key/note columns. Embeds the Self-Exposure Grade ledger so the A-F letter is re-derivable from the published rows. Deterministic sha256 content fingerprint pins an exact bundle for citation.

env.APIFY_TOKEN: read token for GET /v2/datasets/{id}/items. Without it, export-client.js DRY-RUNS and prints the exact request URL it would send (never live, never fabricated rows).

platform_constraints.tabular_limits: csv/xlsx/html capped at 2000 columns, column names <= 200 chars

proxy.capability: Apify Proxy (datacenter + residential, country targeting) — AVAILABILITY-ONLY policy
proxy.summary: Wires Apify Proxy as a compliant, availability-only transport — IP/geo diversity to be a polite client of a PUBLIC page and to render the user's OWN page as their region sees it — and REFUSES evasion. decideProxy() runs an ordered guard pipeline (scope gate -> evasion gate -> group gate -> residential dual-use gate -> coarse-geo gate -> build); the first guard that objects DROPS the request before any fetch (Scrapy DropItem). It READS the real shared/scope.js gate (read-only) so a proxy is only ever built for an accepted subject. The compliance INVERSION of Crawlee: a 401/403/429 block is read as a COMPLIANCE STOP (human review), never a retire-and-rotate-to-fresh-IP loop; at most one retry, transport faults only. RESIDENTIAL is dual-use, restricted to scope=self|public_figure with a written geo_justification; US-state/subdivision targeting is disabled (HIBP minimum-disclosure stance). NO-FAKE-DATA: without APIFY_PROXY_PASSWORD, prepareProxy() dry-runs and returns the REDACTED url it WOULD build (password stripped), makes no network call, and yields no usable proxy; a refused request yields no proxy url at all.

env.APIFY_PROXY_PASSWORD: proxy password from the Apify account; builds the live proxy URL in memory at fetch time. Unset => proxy-client.js DRY-RUNS and returns the redacted url it would build (never logged, never fabricated).

compliance_floor.retire_on_block: block status = COMPLIANCE STOP, not fresh-IP retry loop

optout.capability: Scope-gated DATA-BROKER OPT-OUT workflow (Apify WCC/RAG re-check + paced Schedule + reappearance Webhook)
optout.summary: Turns the subject's OWN confirmed broker listings into (1) a STIX 2.1 Observed Data record, (2) a ready-to-send GDPR Art.17 erasure request, and (3) a PROPOSED paced Apify re-check (Schedule cron + reappearance Webhook). Removing yourself from people-search/data-broker sites (Spokeo/Whitepages/BeenVerified/…) is the most-recommended concrete self-protection step (EFF, Privacy Rights Clearinghouse, California Delete Act/DROP) and the literal OPPOSITE of stalking: a first-person 'remove the listing about ME' request, valid ONLY for the subject's own listing. buildOptOutPlan() runs an ordered fail-closed guard chain: Door 1 = the REAL shared/scope.js gate (read-only) refuses any rejected subject; Door 2 = SELF-ONLY narrowing to scope=self|consented (public_figure/brand/safety_evidence refused — you cannot delete a third party from a broker); then it confirms listings ONLY from REAL module_events whose host matches a broker in the TEMPLATE registry (no fabricated listings — empty in => empty out). It REUSES (never duplicates) shared/enrich/stix-evidence.js for the Observed Data and shared/aux/takedown-letter.js for the erasure draft (a broker host is third-party => GDPR_ERASURE+CCPA_DELETE+SEARCH_DEINDEX). A removed listing that REAPPEARS is re-flagged via a paced re-check whose cron is derived by the REAL integrations/schedules cadence-policy (anti-compulsion floor: higher distress => slower) plus an ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED webhook (integrations/webhooks); the re-check ingests through the EXISTING WCC/RAG path (integrations/ingest, robots forced on, pages/depth clamped). consented is NOT auto-schedulable => manual re-check (fail-closed). NO-FAKE-DATA + deployed:false throughout.
optout.maps_to: shared/detectors module_events -> shared/enrich/stix-evidence (STIX 2.1 Observed Data, OpenCTI/MISP interop) + shared/aux/takedown-letter (GDPR Art.17 erasure)

mcp.capability: Apify MCP tool-server (Model Context Protocol) — gate-enforcing AI-agent surface
mcp.summary: A real, runnable MCP tool layer (mcp/server.js) that exposes the product's EXISTING compliance planners to AI agents as three tools — audit_scope_check, plan_broker_optout, build_takedown_letter — where EVERY handler routes its subject through the REAL shared/scope.js validateScope() FIRST (read-only require) and then REUSES (never re-implements) shared/aux/broker-optout.js, shared/aux/takedown-letter.js and shared/enrich/stix-evidence.js. The single guarantee: an AI-agent caller CANNOT bypass the red lines through the tool layer — a non-self/non-consented subject, a prohibited scope_type, a laundered/stalking freeText, and a private-social host are all rejected by the same gate that guards the web (window.ExDitector.runPolicyGate) and actor paths; on refusal the tool FAILS CLOSED and returns the gate's own refusal, never a result. This is the LOCAL, gate-enforcing counterpart of the remote Apify MCP server's ?tools= whitelist (mcp/client-config.example.json): the whitelist keeps private-scraping actors physically uncallable, and this server keeps prohibited INTENT uncallable. Mirrors the Apify MCP contract (each tool declares a JSON-Schema inputSchema; listTools()=tools/list, callTool()=tools/call; transport moved SSE→Streamable HTTP on 2026-04-01). The transport adapter is intentionally NOT bundled so the registry stays dependency-free and unit-testable. REFERENCE ARCHITECTURES: DeleteMe/Aura broker opt-out (authorization-gated, self-only removal acting only on confirmed listings) for plan_broker_optout, and GDPR Art.17 RTBF erasure-request automation (third-party data-subject erasure channel, template-only) for build_takedown_letter. NO network fetch, NO fabricated data, deployed:false.

tools.audit_scope_check: pre-flight: returns the canonical scope-gate decision only (never footprint data); rejects private-person tracking + laundered stalking prompts
tools.plan_broker_optout: SELF-ONLY data-broker opt-out plan (DeleteMe/Aura pattern) over user-confirmed listings; refused for public_figure/brand/other; reuses buildBrokerOptOutPlan
tools.build_takedown_letter: GDPR Art.17 RTBF / CCPA-delete erasure letters (template) + STIX 2.1 evidence bundle from the user's OWN findings; reuses buildTakedownPlan + toBundle

reuses.scope_gate: shared/scope.js (validateScope, read-only — front-door of every tool)

platform_constraints.tool_whitelist_param: ?tools= on https://mcp.apify.com is the remote compliance control (private-scraping actors absent)
platform_constraints.tool_contract: each tool declares a JSON-Schema inputSchema; tools/list + tools/call

grade.capability: Self-Exposure Grade — a deterministic A+…F letter over the run's REAL module_events (Mozilla HTTP Observatory / SecurityHeaders model)
grade.summary: A single plain number a first-time visitor grasps in a second ('my footprint is a D'). Computed ONLY over the user's already-collected REAL module_events: baseline 100, a frozen table of NAMED per-category weighted deductions (secret leak −45, breach hit −40, broker listing −30, self-published postal −30, …), each scaled by the event's frozen RISK + VISIBILITY rank, with repeat-damping + a per-category cap, mapped onto A+…F bands — exactly the Observatory's baseline-minus-named-penalties shape, reframed from security headers to SELF privacy exposures. Every deduction in the breakdown NAMES the finding that caused it, so the user sees WHICH exposure to remove to raise the grade (Closure-Mode friendly). NO-FAKE-DATA: zero real events ⇒ graded:false / grade:null ('no data, no grade') — never a fabricated 'A' for an unscanned subject. SCOPE-GATED: gradeForScopedRun routes the input through the REAL shared/scope.js (read-only) and grades ONLY an allowed scope=self run; any rejection or non-self scope returns NO grade. The breakdown ledger is the exact reproducible rubric the portable evidence package publishes, so a third party can re-derive the letter from the bundle.

notify.capability: Apify native Integrations (Slack / Make / n8n / Zapier / generic webhook) as a COMPLIANT, Closure-Mode notification dispatch — the audit-result last mile
notify.summary: Apify can fan a run event (ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED/FAILED/TIMED_OUT/ABORTED) out to Slack/Make/n8n/Zapier/generic-webhook with a Handlebars template over the run document — the last mile of how a result leaves the platform. For a normal scraper you'd template a clickable Console/dataset link; for THIS product that is harmful two ways (LEAK: the run doc + dataset carry raw url/storage-key/subject_label; COMPULSION: a real-time 'new change, click here' ping is the slot-machine loop Closure Mode exists to break). decideNotification() is the chokepoint that turns a raw run event into a SAFE, PACED, LINKLESS payload, REUSING (never reimplementing) three existing modules: (1) integrations/exports/redaction-policy.js — every change row is redacted by TLP marking; external channels default to TLP:GREEN and TLP:RED to an external sink is REFUSED, so url/html_key/screenshot_key/subject_label physically cannot leave (only prove-it-changed hashes/flags/timestamps do); (2) integrations/webhooks/output-health.js — success != valid output: an EMPTY/MALFORMED/FAILED/COMPLIANCE-STOP run yields not_ready/compliance_stop and dispatch:false, NEVER a fake 'your audit is ready' (NO-FAKE-DATA); (3) integrations/schedules/cadence-policy.js — the minimum gap between notifications IS the distress-aware anti-compulsion floor (no high-frequency option; higher distress => SLOWER, never faster; a second send inside the floor is suppressed with suppressed_until). The default and most common notification is a calm 'no change — nothing to do' reassurance; a change notification states what + what-next with clickable_target ALWAYS null. PURE: no network I/O, ships no credentials, fabricates no data — it only shapes a payload from REAL inputs and refuses to announce a bad/empty run. The transport (actually POSTing to a platform) is a thin glue an operator wires LAST.

platform_constraints.slack_message_template: Handlebars over the policy payload (NOT the raw run document); 2..4000 chars; mrkdwn-safe; NO Console/dataset link templated

reference_architectures.observatory_securityheaders: grade LETTER as the calm headline, not per-event alarms (https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/observatory/docs/tests_and_scoring, https://securityheaders.com/)
reference_architectures.govuk_design_system: green notification-banner / confirmation-page voice — confirm the expected thing happened, use sparingly, state what-next (https://design-system.service.gov.uk/components/notification-banner/, https://design-system.service.gov.uk/patterns/confirmation-pages/)

module_selftest_aggregator.purpose: Discovers & spawns every *_selftest.js in the repo (each in its own node process) so the ~dozen real module self-tests are guarded by CI — the default npm test (test/run-compliance-tests.js, Codex-owned) only checks scope-rejection fixtures. Additive + collision-safe: does NOT modify test/ or package.json.

compliance.notes: No private-individual tracking, no romance/intimacy/gender inference, no private-social scraping, no login/captcha/rate-limit evasion. Dual-use enumeration only for scope=self|public_figure via validateScope.
